Rename package from com.dkvs to org.dkvs

Fix histogram metrics with programmatic MeterFilter instead of YAML property

The YAML percentile-histogram property didn't bind correctly for dotted
meter names. Use a MeterFilter bean to reliably enable histogram buckets
for http.server.requests.

Replace manual getters/setters with Lombok in Url entity

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Random assembly animation: rows appear in shuffled order

Each row gets a deterministic-random delay so the tower
assembles chaotically instead of linearly bottom-to-top.
Duration increased to 0.7s for smoother appearance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Responsive fix + slower animation: fill screen on any resolution

- Tower width factor 0.55→0.68 for wider layout on square screens
- Scale-UP after layout: grow tower to fill 82% of container height
- Scale-DOWN still applies if tower overflows 92%
- Cap svgHeight to containerHeight*0.92 to prevent overflow
- Animation: 0.4s→0.6s duration, 100ms→150ms stagger per row

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove JS text truncation in table formatters, let CSS line-clamp handle it

Removed the 60-char details truncation and 24-char secret truncation from
JS formatters — CSS 3-line clamp handles visual truncation, and the removed
JS truncation was preventing tooltip overflow detection from triggering.
Also improved tooltip handler with dual detection: JS truncation check
(tip length vs visible text) and CSS clamp check (unset clamp to measure
true scrollHeight).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix tooltip overflow detection for line-clamped cells

Previous scrollHeight check didn't work with -webkit-line-clamp.
Now temporarily unsets the clamp to measure true content height,
then compares against clamped height to decide if tooltip is needed.

commit.message:
Fix graph ordering: WEB01 after DC01, access→host edges

Pivot edges now prefer host→host (not access→host) so BFS layer
assignment places pivot destinations after the source host. Added
unconditional access→host edges so the chain flows correctly:
cred → access → host → [pivot] → next host.

Fix graph: WEB01 as pivot dest, table cell wrapping + tooltips

  pivotDests built from raw destination text that never matched hosts)
- Fuzzy pivot source/destination matching: descriptive text like
  "DC01.pirate.htb (10.129.244.95) - gMSA..." now matches host IPs
  and subnet prefixes (192.168.100.0/24 matches host 192.168.100.2)

- Pivot edge labels truncated to first segment (≤40 chars)

commit.message:
Add via_access_id provenance tracking to kill-chain graph

Schema v3→v4 migration adds via_access_id FK on credentials and vulns
tables, linking discoveries to the access session that found them.
State-server add_credential/add_vuln accept the new parameter.
State-viewer graph uses via_access_id for explicit edge inference
(access→vuln, access→cred) with heuristic fallback for NULL values.
Access nodes are pre-created before vuln/cred processing so references
resolve correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: red-run
repository.description: Offensive security toolkit for Claude Code

commit.message:
Fix provided-cred chain ordering and reduce edge crossings

Provided-cred chain detection was fragile — parsed node labels to
find hosts. Now tracks hostsViaProvidedCred directly during access
node creation when matching against a provided credential. This
ensures attacker->cred->access->host->downstream ordering instead
of cred appearing as a sibling of the host.

Also broadened cred->access matching to check access.method for
the credential username (catches cases where username formats
differ between cred and access records).

Added barycenter heuristic for node ordering within layers (3
passes). Nodes in each layer are sorted by the average Y position
of their parent nodes, which reduces edge crossings — e.g., the
DC->gMSA_ADCS edge no longer has to route through credential boxes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Keep them in the Blocked table only. Removes blocked node creation,

repository.name: red-run
repository.description: Offensive security toolkit for Claude Code

commit.message:
Fix kill-chain ordering: provided creds flow attacker->cred->access->host

When a credential is provided (source matches "pentest", "provided",
etc.), the chain now flows: Attacker -> cred -> access -> host ->
downstream findings. Previously the host got a direct edge from
Attacker, making the cred appear as a sibling instead of an ancestor.

Changes:
- Skip attacker->host edge for hosts reachable via provided-cred chain
- Add access->host bridge edges so downstream nodes (vulns, pivots,
  blocked) that connect from the host node stay in the chain

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: red-run
repository.description: Offensive security toolkit for Claude Code

commit.message:
Prune dead-end vulns from graph, link provided creds from Attacker

Graph now only shows vulns that chain into something downstream
(credential, access). Dead-end findings that didn't lead anywhere
are pruned from the graph but remain in the Vulns table.

Credentials whose source matches "provided", "scope", "pentest",
"given", "initial", or "pre-engagement" now link from the Attacker
node instead of from a host — reflecting that they were supplied
as part of scope, not discovered through exploitation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.description: Offensive security toolkit for Claude Code

commit.message:
Render pivots as labeled edges instead of nodes

Pivots are relationships, not entities — they now render as styled
edges between source access/host and destination host. Edge label
shows method + status (e.g. "sshuttle", "chisel (identified)").
Exploited pivots are solid green, identified are dashed yellow,
blocked are dotted red. Removes the uninformative column of
identical "pivot" boxes.

Edge legend labels updated: Exploited/Identified/Blocked.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.description: Offensive security toolkit for Claude Code

commit.message:
Improve kill-chain graph: subtitles, legend, manual refresh

Graph nodes now show two lines — label + contextual subtitle:
- Hosts: IP + role/OS
- Vulns: title + severity (color-coded by severity level)
- Credentials: user + secret_type (cracked)
- Access: user@host + type|privilege
- Pivots: method + source->destination (instead of generic "pivot")
- Blocked: technique + truncated reason

Adds a legend bar below the graph showing node shapes/colors and
edge styles (confirmed/pending/blocked).

SSE auto-refresh now only updates cards + tables. Graph only redraws
on explicit Refresh button click to prevent disruptive reloads.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add state-viewer web dashboard with kill-chain graph

Read-only browser dashboard for engagement/state.db served at localhost:8099.
Stdlib-only Python HTTP server with inline HTML/CSS/JS — no dependencies.

Features:
- Kill-chain SVG attack graph (hosts, vulns, creds, access, pivots, blocked)
- State tables with sorting, filtering, collapsible sections
- SSE live updates (events every 2s, full refresh every 10s)
- Token-based auth for remote access (generate-token.sh binds 0.0.0.0)
- HMAC-signed session cookies, Bearer header support for API clients
- Graceful degradation when DB doesn't exist yet

Closes #64

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Handle TypeError from GrovePi I2C and add startup delay

commit.message:
Move workflows to .github/workflows/ with #@dist, add dry-run test suite (#1841)

* fix: install project dependencies before agentic-step runs tests

The dev and fix-stuck jobs run agentic-step with test-command: "npm test",
but only installed the action's own dependencies, not the project's
devDependencies (vitest). This caused "vitest: not found" errors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Move distributable workflows to .github/workflows/ with #@dist markers

Workflows now live in .github/workflows/agentic-lib-*.yml (was src/workflows/
and src/seeds/) with commented-out triggers and dry-run defaults. During init,
the applyDistTransform() function restores triggers and production defaults.

Root agentic-lib.toml replaces zero-agentic-lib.toml — paths are prefixed with
test/ locally and transformed to standard paths for distribution. Fizz-buzz
test fixtures in test/ provide sandboxed content for workflow dry-runs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add workflow dry-run tests to test.yml — exercise all 5 distributable workflows

test.yml now calls all 5 distributable workflows in inert mode:
- agentic-lib-workflow: 3 concurrent modes (maintain, review, dev) with dry-run
- agentic-lib-bot: responds to test discussion #1840
- agentic-lib-schedule: edits files but skips commit/push (new dry-run input)
- agentic-lib-init: runs init with dry-run, skips commit/push (new workflow_call + dry-run gates)
- agentic-lib-test: runs npm test on the checkout

Also adds workflow_call trigger to bot and init workflows, and dry-run
input to schedule workflow. Fixes tests/template/agentic-lib.toml
references to use root agentic-lib.toml.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix workflow dry-run tests: self-init, conditional checkout, sub-project deps

- Add self-init step to workflow/bot: detects agentic-lib dev environment
  (has scripts/self-init.sh but no .github/agentic-lib/) and copies
  src/ content into place before agentic-step runs
- Schedule/init: conditional checkout — use branch ref in dry-run,
  ref: main in production (fixes ENOENT on branch-only files)
- Init: fallback to local bin/agentic-lib.js when npx package unavailable
- Test: install sub-project deps when running inside agentic-lib

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: enhance git pull logic and manage concurrency settings in workflows

* Fix test-cli-maintain-library: seed test/ paths matching agentic-lib.toml

The test was seeding SOURCES.md at repo root but the CLI reads from
test/SOURCES.md per agentic-lib.toml config. Added a URL to test/SOURCES.md
and updated test steps to seed test/ paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Dogfood init --purge for CLI test seeding, add URL to seed SOURCES.md

CLI test jobs now use `node bin/agentic-lib.js init --purge --mission
fizz-buzz --target /tmp/test-workspace` instead of hand-rolling seed
steps. This exercises the real init pipeline end-to-end and ensures
paths match the transformed agentic-lib.toml config.

Also adds a fizz-buzz Wikipedia URL to zero-SOURCES.md and test/SOURCES.md
so maintain-library has content to process.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix CLI test seeding: mkdir target directory before init --purge

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add 6 init pipeline tests: idempotency, seeded npm test, YAML validity,
update mode, stale cleanup, all missions

New deterministic tests (no Copilot token needed):
- test-init-idempotency: init --purge twice produces no diff
- test-seeded-npm-test: freshly seeded workspace passes npm test
- test-dist-yaml-validity: #@dist transform produces valid YAML
- test-init-update-mode: update preserves user content, refreshes infra,
  verifies distributed toml matches source defaults
- test-stale-workflow-cleanup: stale workflows are removed by init
- test-all-missions-seed: all 11 mission seeds complete successfully

Also adds scripts/validate-dist-transform.js for reusable transform
validation (YAML + toml marker check + toml compare).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix test failures: strip agentic-lib dep before npm install, mkdir features

- test-cli-transform: strip @xn-intenton-z2a/agentic-lib dependency
  from seeded package.json before npm install (package is on npmjs.org
  but CI npmrc redirects scope to GitHub Packages)
- test-seeded-npm-test: same fix
- test-init-update-mode: mkdir features/ before writing custom.md
  (init --purge doesn't create features/ directory)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Drop action transform tests, keep CLI tests only

Removes 3 action-based LLM test jobs (test-maintain-features,
test-maintain-library, test-transform) that duplicated the CLI tests.
Saves ~5min and 3 LLM round-trips per CI run. The CLI tests exercise
the same Copilot SDK logic through the CLI invocation path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement task 59: lazy binding evaluation for MATCH patterns

- Add match_patterns_for_binding(): chains one Bindings through all patterns
  sequentially, short-circuiting when any step yields zero results
- Change execute_match_clause() to use per-binding lazy expansion instead of
  batch processing all bindings through each pattern in turn; reduces peak
  memory from O(M^N) to O(M*K) for N-pattern queries with M matches each
- Add early LIMIT termination in execute_match(): when no ORDER BY and no
  aggregation, stop collecting bindings once LIMIT+SKIP rows are found
- Add 3 tests: early-termination limit, multi-pattern correctness, aggregation
  correctness (count must still see all rows despite LIMIT 1)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: make dialog content scrollable when taller than viewport

Move dialog positioning from fixed+translate centering to a scrollable
overlay flex container. Uses my-auto for vertical centering of short
dialogs while allowing tall ones to scroll from the top.

feat: worktree-aware pruning + honest skip reporting (#149)

Prune now handles branches checked out in worktrees:
- Detects "used by worktree" errors when git branch -D fails
- Safely removes the worktree first (no --force), then retries
- Refuses to remove worktrees with uncommitted changes

Fixes misleading "No stale branches" when branches are skipped:
- Shows "Skipped N branch(es)" when deleted=0 but skipped>0
- Shows combined "Pruned N, skipped M" when both are non-zero

commit.message:
fix: mostrar suma de variaciones en Stock MeLi del parent — no solo la variación matcheada

Para items con múltiples variaciones, avail_qty era el stock de la variación
que coincidía con el SKU del parent, ignorando las demás. Si esa variación
tenía 0 (ej: Base De Mesa), el parent mostraba 0 aunque otra variación
(Base De Pared) tuviera 8 unidades.

Ahora: avail_qty = sum(v.available_quantity for v in variations)
Refleja el stock real disponible del listing completo.

Ejemplo MLM1917424611: Pared=8 + Mesa=0 → muestra 8 (correcto).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix installer: remove rm that was deleting .env file

rm -f .env'' in bash expands to rm -f .env ('' is an empty string),
which deleted the .env file after generating all secrets and port
config. This was the root cause of all port detection failures —
docker compose ran with no .env and fell back to default port 3000.

commit.message:
Isolate D_c connectivity blocker and add direct numerical support

In BHWTranslation.lean:
- bhw_translation_invariant is now sorry-free, delegating to the new
  named lemma isConnected_permutedExtendedTube_inter_translate which
  captures exactly the missing geometric premise for the identity-theorem
  argument on D = PET ∩ (PET - c).
- Remove stale hPET_conn and with hD_def (unused after proof refactor).
- Update module comment to reflect the correct 4-helper structure.
- Add forwardTube_inter_translate_nonempty (fully proved): for any c,
  there exists z ∈ FT with z+c ∈ FT (used as anchor for identity theorem).
- Direct numerical connectivity test run for d=1,n=2 across 9 shift
  values (including stress tests with Im(c) up to 0.8i): ALL returned
  1 connected component (largest_frac=1.000). Docstring updated accordingly.

In PermutationFlowBlocker.lean:
- Update blocker_isConnected_permSeedSet_nontrivial docstring: replace
  mixed-result wording with refined campaign outcome (all 80 trials
  SUPPORTED) and explain the sampling-artifact root cause of the earlier
  apparent falsifier.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(rpc): XML-escape string values in RPC request builders (#76)

Add xml_escape() helper to sanitize user-provided strings (URLs,
names, emails, passwords, filenames) before inserting them into
XML format!() templates. Prevents malformed or dangerous requests
when values contain &, <, >, " or ' characters.

Uses single-pass character iteration for efficiency. Escaping
applied to 9 methods: result_op, project_op, transfer_op,
lookup_account, project_attach, get_project_config, create_account,
acct_mgr_rpc, set_language. XML tags and serialized XML blocks
(prefs, proxy, cc_config) are not escaped.
